title: About the Business Operations Survey | Stats NZ
content:
## About the Business Operations Survey
17 April 2026, 2:00pm
The Business Operations Survey was paused in 2024. A new national Survey of Business Operations is being jointly delivered by Ministry of Business, Innovation and Employment in partnership with Stats NZ in 2026.
The new survey builds on the Business Operations Survey, retaining core questions to ensure comparability over time.
New questions have also been added to address emerging areas, such as use and impact of artificial intelligence (AI).
Learn more about the Survey of Business Operations
The Business of Operations survey was started in 2005. It was last carried out in 2023, and was paused for 2024 and 2025.
This survey collected information about how New Zealand businesses operate. It helped to build a better understanding of practices and behaviour of businesses that may affect the business performance of each industry.
In 2023, approximately 9,000 New Zealand businesses took part in this survey. They were selected from the 48,000 businesses that employ six or more staff and that are engaged in the following industries:
- Agriculture, forestry, and fishing
- Electricity, gas, water, and waste services
- Transport, postal, and warehousing
- Information media and telecommunications
- Financial and insurance services
- Rental, hiring, and real estate services
- Professional, scientific, and technical services
- Administrative and support services
- Education and training
- Health care and social assistance
- Arts and recreation services.
The questions included in this survey were asked under the Data and Statistics Act 2022.
Business Operations Survey 2023 is a sample of the 2023 questionnaire.
Our approach to survey compliance has more information about the legal obligation to participate in the Business Operations Survey.

## Results from previous surveys
Business operations survey: 2023 information release has results from the most recent survey.
